examples:
Command Example
!msgraph-calendar-create-event user="someemail@domain.com" calendar_id="Calendar" attendees="someemail@domain.com" body="This event was created by MSGraph integration" subject="test - delete" location="Tel Aviv" end="2022-12-11T17:30:00" start="2022-12-11T17:00:00" time_zone="Asia/Jerusalem"
Context Example
{
"MSGraphCalendar.Event": [
{
"@Odata.Context": "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/$metadata#users('oren%40demistodev.onmicrosoft.com')/calendars('Calendar')/events/$entity",
"Attendees": [
name: msgraph-calendar-create-event

outputs.description: The start time zone that was set when the event was created. MSGraphCalendar.Event.HasAttachments Whether the event has attachments.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.OriginalStartTimeZone

outputs.description: Whether the sender would like a response when the event is accepted or declined. MSGraphCalendar.Event.WebLink The URL used to open the event in Outlook on the web.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.ResponseRequested

outputs.description: The recurrence pattern for the event. MSGraphCalendar.Event.Start The date, time, and time zone that the event starts. By default, the start time is in UTC.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.Recurrence

outputs.description: The starting date, time, and time zone of the event. By default, the start time is in UTC. MSGraphCalendar.Event.Location The location of the event.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.End

outputs.description: The collection of attendees for the event. MSGraphCalendar.Event.Type The event type. For example, singleInstance, occurrence, exception, seriesMaster. Read-only.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.Attendees

outputs.description: Indicates the type of response sent in response to an event message. MSGraphCalendar.Event.Importance The importance of the event. For example, low, normal, high.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.ResponseStatus.response

outputs.description: A unique identifier that is shared by all instances of an event across different calendars. MSGraphCalendar.Event.IsCancelled Whether the event has been canceled.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.ICalUId

outputs.description: Whether the event lasts all day. MSGraphCalendar.Event.ReminderMinutesBeforeStart The number of minutes before the event start time of the reminder alert.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.IsAllDay

outputs.description: Last modified time and date of the event using ISO 8601 format in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is '2014-01-01T00:00:00Z' MSGraphCalendar.Event.OriginalEndTimeZone The end time zone that was set when the event was created.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.LastModifiedDateTime

outputs.description: The time and date the event was created using ISO 8601 format in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is '2014-01-01T00:00:00Z' MSGraphCalendar.Event.ChangeKey Identifies the version of the event object. Every time the event is changed, the ChangeKey changes as well. This allows the exchange to apply changes to the correct version of the object.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.CreatedDateTime

outputs.description: The event's ID. MSGraphCalendar.Event.IsOrganizer Whether the message sender is also the organizer.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.ID

outputs.description: The sensitivity of the event. Possible values are: normal, personal, private, confidential. MSGraphCalendar.Event.IsReminderOn Whether an alert is set to remind the user of the event.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.Sensitivity

outputs.description: The organizer of the event. MSGraphCalendar.Event.Subject The text of the event's subject line.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.Organizer

outputs.description: A URL for an online meeting, which is used only when an organizer specifies an event as an online meeting, such as a Skype meeting. Read-only.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Event.OnlineMeetingUrl
